nativeRange.habitat: Found among coral heads and rocks in subtidal lagoons and seaward reefs, at depths of roughly 1 to 55 m.

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding primarily on filamentous green and red algae, tunicates, small amounts of corals, bryozoans, polychaetes, echinoderms, molluscs, and brown and coralline red algae. In captivity tends to accept a varied meaty diet of mysis, krill, squid, clams, and chopped seafood; hard-shelled foods such as clams on the half-shell or small shrimp pieces are generally advised 2–3 times per week to help wear down the species' continuously growing beak-like fused teeth.

summary: The Saddle Valentini Puffer (Canthigaster valentini) is a small Indo-Pacific toby, reaching around 10 cm, with striking dark saddle markings, orange-brown spots, and blue striping. It is generally considered hardy but tends to nip invertebrates and corals, making it best suited to fish-only setups.
careNotes:
Canthigaster valentini is one of the widely distributed Indo-Pacific sharpnose puffers, ranging from the Red Sea east to the Tuamotu Islands and north to southern Japan. Adults are commonly reported at 10–11 cm and bear four dark saddle-like bands, orange-brown spotting on the lower body, blue striping along the back, and yellow fins.

This species is famously mimicked by the filefish Paraluteres prionurus (the mimic filefish), which closely resembles Canthigaster valentini and gains protection from predators that have learned to avoid the puffer's toxic flesh. The puffer itself contains tetrodotoxin and related compounds in its skin, viscera and gonads; it is poisonous to eat, and like other tetraodontids it may release skin-borne toxins under severe stress or at death — a small risk that nevertheless places it in the broad 'potentially tank-toxic' pufferfish category, particularly in tightly enclosed systems with sensitive tankmates.

Males are territorial and often fight other Valentinis as well as other Canthigaster tobies; the species is best kept singly unless a confirmed mated pair is obtained for a large system. It has been reported to nip at sessile invertebrates, soft corals, polyps, ornamental shrimp, small snails and hermit crabs, so it is generally considered reef-safe only with caution and is most reliably housed in fish-only or FOWLR setups.

A 150 L tank with ample rockwork, open swimming space and stable water chemistry is a reasonable practical minimum.

Like all puffers, it should be moved using a water-filled container rather than a net to avoid inflation with air, which can be fatal. Its continuously growing beak benefits from regular hard-shelled foods.
breedingNotes: Wild reproduction involves male territory defence and female-guarding behaviour; demersal eggs are laid in algal tufts. Captive breeding of Canthigaster valentini is not commonly documented and is generally considered impractical in standard home-aquarium settings.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care sheet for the Saddle Valentini Puffer: confirmed scientific name, 4-inch adult size, 30-gallon minimum tank, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, peaceful temperament with reef-safe-with-caution status, and diet recommendations. Designated collection URL (https://www.liveaquaria.com/collections/puffers) returned 404; navigated from the collections index to the species product page.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Tetraodontidae, Tetraodontiformes), max length (11 cm TL), wild temperature range (24.6–28.9°C, mean 27.6), depth (1–55 m), Indo-Pacific distribution, omnivorous diet, mimicry by Paraluteres prionurus, and tetrodotoxin-bearing status. Wild temperature mean is higher than retailer 72–78°F figures; averaged across sources to produce final 23–27°C range.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (10 cm), 30-gallon minimum tank, temperature 23–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, semi-aggressive temperament (disagreed with LiveAquaria's 'peaceful'; the middle 'semi-aggressive' value was taken given documented conspecific aggression and fin-nipping reports), lifespan up to ~5 years, and 'no/with caution' reef-safe status.

fish.breedingNotes: Not commonly bred in captivity. Wild spawning involves male territoriality and demersal eggs laid in algal tufts; no widely documented home-aquarium breeding protocol.
